title: westoniella eriocephala
intro: Westoniella eriocephala, also known as Western Stickseed, is a flowering plant native to Western North America, characterized by its yellow flowers and stick-like seeds. Traditionally, Western Stickseed has been used in herbal medicine for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, making it effective in treating various health conditions. The medicinal properties of Westoniella eriocephala have been known to provide relief from digestive issues, wounds, and skin irritations, offering health benefits such as improved digestion, reduced inflammation, and accelerated wound healing. Westoniella eriocephala can be prepared into teas, infusions, or salves, allowing for various herbal preparations to be utilized depending on the desired health benefits.
